_notes: L-E cell (SPEC_V1X): the supersession policy's own loses-cell, ratified standing rule 2. A history question whose answer IS the superseded record. The store still holds it (append-only) and naive retrieval serves it; the policy-on lane buries it because the boundary cannot know the question wants history. The burial cost made visible — this cell also exercises exactly the failure mode the gate-order fix prevents from happening accidentally (codex/kagi). AMENDED pre-run: correction text no longer mentions vega-12 — leaking the original hostname into the superseding record would let the policy lane answer the history question and defeat the cell. AMENDED pre-run 2: records reordered to created_at order (the ranker's recency proxy is append order) and recency_weight=0 — the history question's similarity alone must rank the plan into naive offers; first wire run showed recency drowning it.
question: For the postmortem record: what host did the ORIGINAL Thursday migration plan specify for api-gateway, before any later updates? Answer with the hostname only.

records.text: Plan for Thursday: migrate the api-gateway service to host vega-12 during the 14:00-15:00 maintenance window.
Cafeteria menu for Thursday: lentil soup, flatbread, seasonal fruit.
Update to Thursday's migration: after the capacity review, the api-gateway target host is now vega-15. Window unchanged.
